name: WEX Fleet & Payments API
what_it_does: WEX Fleet and Payments REST API for fleet card management, fuel expense tracking, and corporate payment solutions. Enables AI agents to manage fleet card issuance and controls for fleet spend management, handle fuel transaction authorization and exception management for fuel expense control, access driver and vehicle assignment for fleet cost allocation, retrieve fuel price analytics and budget variance for fleet economics, manage fleet card controls (fuel type restriction, purchase controls) for fleet policy enforcement, handle health and benefits payment (HSA/FSA/HRA) management for benefit payment automation, access corporate travel and virtual card management for business expense control, retrieve IFTA fuel tax reporting for commercial vehicle compliance, manage EV charging network integration for electric fleet management, and integrate WEX with TMS, fleet management, and ERP platforms.
use_cases: Managing fleet card spend from fleet management agents
Enforcing fuel purchase controls from policy compliance agents
Processing HSA/FSA payments from benefits payment agents
Reporting IFTA fuel taxes from compliance agents
not_for: Consumer payment cards without fleet or benefits context
Personal expense management without corporate card focus
best_when: A fleet operator, benefits administrator, or corporate travel program using WEX wants AI agents to automate fleet card management, fuel expense control, HSA/FSA payment processing, and IFTA compliance.
avoid_when: PCI DSS PAYMENT CARD DATA: WEX processes payment card data subject to PCI DSS compliance; automated WEX API integrations handling cardholder data require PCI DSS scope assessment; automated card data storage creates PCI DSS compliance obligation. IFTA fuel tax accuracy — automated IFTA reporting must accurately calculate miles by jurisdiction and fuel purchased by state; automated IFTA with incorrect mileage or fuel allocation creates state tax audit and penalty risk. HSA/FSA substantiation requirement — automated HSA/FSA payment processing must comply with IRS substantiation requirements; automated payments for ineligible expenses create IRS excise tax and plan disqualification risk. Fleet card compromise fraud response — automated fleet card controls must respond quickly to suspected fraud; delayed automated response to compromised fleet card creates financial loss from fraudulent fuel purchases.

auth.notes: WEX uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes via WEX Developer Portal. REST API with JSON. Developer portal at developer.wexinc.com. South Portland, Maine HQ. NYSE: WEX. Founded 1983. Public company. Revenue: $2.7B+. 19M+ payment solutions accounts. Fleet cards, benefits payments, and travel payments. Competes with FleetCor (Corpay), Comdata, and FLEETCOR for commercial fleet cards.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise partnership required for API access
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise partnership pricing; interchange-based fee structure
pricing.notes: South Portland, Maine. NYSE: WEX. Founded 1983. Public. Revenue $2.7B+. 19M+ accounts. Fleet and benefits payments. Competes with FleetCor and Comdata.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; payment processing has strict SLA

known_agent_gotchas: PCI DSS CARD DATA COMPLIANCE: WEX API handles payment card data requiring PCI DSS compliance; automated integrations storing or processing cardholder data expand PCI scope; use WEX tokenization and avoid card data storage outside WEX PCI environment

IFTA multi-state jurisdiction accuracy — automated IFTA reporting must correctly allocate miles and fuel purchases by jurisdiction; automated mileage tracking errors by state create IFTA audit and penalty risk across multiple states

HSA/FSA IRS substantiation — automated HSA/FSA payments must meet IRS substantiation requirements for eligible expenses (Section 213(d)); automated payment of ineligible expenses creates IRS excise tax, plan disqualification, and employee tax liability

Fleet card fraud response automation — automated fraud detection on fleet card transactions must respond immediately; compromised fleet cards with delayed automated freeze create significant fraudulent fuel purchase exposure

Fleet card controls granularity — automated fleet card purchase controls (product type, dollar amount, transactions per day) must be configured at appropriate policy level; overly restrictive automated controls create legitimate business disruption

EV charging network integration complexity — automated WEX EV charging integration requires understanding of charging network billing models (time-based vs kWh-based); automated EV charging expense processing must handle multiple charging network billing formats
agent_readiness.security_notes: Fleet and payments. SOC2, PCI-DSS, ISO27001. OAuth2 with scopes. US/EU. Payment card and fleet data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Fleet and payments. SOC2, PCI-DSS, ISO27001. OAuth2 with scopes. US/EU. Payment card and fleet data.
